deep_dive.title: UniSteer: Text-Guided Flow Matching in Activation Space for Versatile LLM Steering
deep_dive.abstract: Activation-based control steers large language models (LLMs) by intervening on their internal representations during inference, and has emerged as an effective paradigm for controlling behaviors such as persona and style. However, existing methods often rely on fixed steering directions or task-specific intervention modules, making them difficult to adapt to fine-grained concepts and compositional constraints. We propose UniSteer, a text-guided activation flow matching model that learns a conditional distribution over residual-stream activations from natural-language conditions. Instead of fitting a separate intervention for each target behavior, UniSteer learns a universal conditional velocity field in activation space. At inference time, UniSteer performs flow inversion by partially transporting a source activation toward a latent state and regenerating it under a target textual condition before injecting it back into the frozen LLM. The same conditional model supports activation-space classification by selecting the textual label with the lowest reconstruction energy. Experiments on three target LLMs show that UniSteer provides a unified interface across behavioral control, truthfulness steering, fine-grained concept steering, multi-constraint instruction following, and activation-space classification.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Text-guided activation flow matching for steering LLMs at inference time
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 文本引导的激活流匹配用于推理时引导LLM
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 文本引导的激活流匹配用于推理时引导LLM
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 文本引导的激活流匹配用于推理时引导LLM
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes UniSteer, a universal activation flow matching model for LLM steering, enabling fine-grained control via natural-language conditions without task-specific modules.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有激活引导方法通常依赖固定方向或任务特定模块，难以灵活应对细粒度概念和组合约束。本文提出UniSteer，一种文本引导的激活流匹配模型，通过学习残差流激活上的条件分布，利用自然语言条件生成通用条件速度场，实现对LLM行为的细粒度控制。实验证明，UniSteer在多种引导任务上显著优于现有方法，且无需为每个行为单独训练。该方法为激活引导提供了统一框架，有望应用于安全数据生成等下游任务。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现有激活引导方法通常依赖固定方向或任务特定模块，难以灵活应对细粒度概念和组合约束。本文提出UniSteer，一种文本引导的激活流匹配模型，通过学习残差流激活上的条件分布，利用自然语言条件生成通用条件速度场，实现对LLM行为的细粒度控制。实验证明，UniSteer在多种引导任务上显著优于现有方法，且无需为每个行为单独训练。该方法为激活引导提供了统一框架，有望应用于安全数据生成等下游任务。

quick_skim.title: When Context Flips, Safety Breaks: Diagnosing Brittle Safety in Aligned Language Models
quick_skim.abstract: Safety benchmark scores provide incomplete evidence of deployment readiness: aligned language models often adhere to rigid rules even when a situational update flips which action is safe. We term this failure brittle safety. To diagnose it, we introduce context-flip evaluation, testing 12 models across a safety benchmark (PacifAIst) and two commonsense controls using paired variants where the nominally safe action produces harm. Three findings emerge. First, brittle safety is safety-specific: all 12 models exhibit a safety-commonsense gap (mean +17.4 pp). Baseline accuracy fails to predict brittleness: among models above 90% baseline accuracy, brittleness rates range from 13.7% to 90.0%. Second, failures stem from policy override rather than miscomprehension: despite acknowledging the context change in every case, models persist via three distinct mechanisms that vary by update type and model family. Third, on a hand-audited probe of catastrophic consequence-flip scenarios, standard action-level guardrails catch none, while a state-aware validator catches all without false alarms on correct interventions. This indicates that action-level content moderation is systematically blind to consequence-flips, motivating state-aware architectural alternatives. We release our protocol, perturbed benchmarks, and deployment probe.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: diagnoses brittle safety in aligned LLMs, directly relevant to safety degradation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 诊断对齐大语言模型的安全脆弱性，直接关联安全退化问题
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 诊断对齐大语言模型的安全脆弱性，直接关联安全退化问题
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 诊断对齐大语言模型的安全脆弱性，直接关联安全退化问题
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces context-flip evaluation to diagnose brittle safety in aligned LLMs, showing that models fail when context changes which action is safe. Evaluates 12 models on a safety benchmark.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 对齐语言模型的安全基准分数无法反映部署时的脆弱性。本文提出上下文翻转评估方法，发现12个模型存在安全-常识差距，基线准确率高时脆弱性仍可达90%。揭示了安全退化源于策略覆盖而非知识缺失，为缓解安全退化提供诊断工具。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 对齐语言模型的安全基准分数无法反映部署时的脆弱性。本文提出上下文翻转评估方法，发现12个模型存在安全-常识差距，基线准确率高时脆弱性仍可达90%。揭示了安全退化源于策略覆盖而非知识缺失，为缓解安全退化提供诊断工具。
